title: Writing - Part 1: Taking Action on Climate Change
description: Your class has been discussing what individuals and governments can do to tackle climate change.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the actions in your notes. You should explain which action you think is more effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Small lifestyle changes by millions of people can add up to a significant reduction in emissions.
Strict laws on pollution and emissions force companies to change their practices quickly.
Switching to solar and wind energy is the most sustainable long-term solution.

structure.introduction: Introduce the urgency of climate change and the need for action.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first action and how it can help reduce the impact of climate change.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second action and argue which of the two is more effective.
structure.conclusion: Summarise both actions and give your final opinion.

content.modelAnswer:
Climate change is one of the greatest challenges facing humanity today. While there are many ways to address it, two approaches stand out: encouraging individuals to reduce their carbon footprint and investing in renewable energy.

Reducing personal carbon footprints through choices such as using public transport, eating less meat and saving energy can make a real difference if adopted widely. However, relying solely on individual action is slow and inconsistent, as not everyone is willing or able to make these changes.

In contrast, investing in renewable energy tackles the problem at its root. By replacing fossil fuels with solar and wind power on a large scale, governments can drastically cut emissions without depending on every citizen to act. This systemic approach creates lasting infrastructure that benefits future generations.

In conclusion, although individual actions are valuable, I believe renewable energy investment is the more effective strategy. A structural shift in how we produce energy is essential for achieving the reductions needed to protect our planet.
